tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P1] Manifest `storage` block Zod schema (StorageMigration + StorageDeclaration) + tests
tasks.description: Add the storage-declaration schemas to packages/extension-api/src/manifest-schema.ts. Export StorageMigrationSchema = z.object({ version: z.number().int().positive(), name: z.string().optional(), up: z.string().min(1) }).strict() and StorageDeclarationSchema = z.object({ quotaBytes: z.number().int().positive().optional(), migrations: z.array(StorageMigrationSchema) }).strict().refine(s => s.migrations.every((m,i)=>m.version===i+1), 'storage.migrations must be numbered 1..N in order with no gaps'). Add `storage: StorageDeclarationSchema.optional()` to ExtensionManifestSchema (currently lines 90-121). TSDoc on every export (Hard Rule 4). Export the inferred types (StorageMigration, StorageDeclaration). ACCEPTANCE: schema accepts a valid 1..N migration array (with and without quotaBytes/name) and a manifest with no storage block; rejects version gaps ([1,3]), duplicates ([1,1]), zero/negative versions, empty `up`, and unknown keys on BOTH the declaration and each migration object (.strict() on both schemas). Add __tests__/manifest-storage-schema.test.ts, each case a purpose comment, no always-pass. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run packages/extension-api/src/__tests__/manifest-storage-schema.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/extension-api typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 3 (schema declaration). No dependencies; parallel with 1.2, 2.1, 2.3, 4.3.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the manifest storage-declaration schema and tests

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P1] Extract shared scope-aware path resolver (resolveExtensionDataDir / resolveBlobPath / resolveDbPath) + golden test
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-data-paths.ts. Extract the scope logic from resolveDataPath (apps/server/src/routes/extensions.ts:537-553) into resolveExtensionDataDir(id, manager, dorkHome, getCwd): string | null returning the scope-aware directory (local extension record.scope==='local' → path.join(cwd, '.dork', 'extension-data', id); global → path.join(dorkHome, 'extension-data', id); null when record missing or cwd unresolvable). Add resolveBlobPath(...) = join(dir, 'data.json') and resolveDbPath(...) = join(dir, 'store.db'). TSDoc on every export. Refactor routes/extensions.ts to import resolveBlobPath in place of the inlined resolveDataPath (delete the private copy) — NO behavior change to the blob routes. os.homedir() banned (Hard Rule 3): dorkHome is threaded in. ACCEPTANCE: resolveBlobPath returns byte-identical paths to the pre-refactor resolveDataPath for both global and local scopes (golden test comparing against the known-good strings); resolveDbPath returns the sibling store.db; null for a missing record and for a local extension with no cwd; SAFE_EXT_ID rejection is unchanged at the route. Add __tests__/extension-data-paths.test.ts. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-data-paths.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 1 (path resolution extracted and shared). No dependencies; parallel with 1.1, 2.1, 2.3, 4.3.
tasks.activeForm: Extracting the shared scope-aware extension data-path resolver

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P1] ExtensionDatabase: per-extension connection (house pragmas) + _dork_meta bootstrap + quota measurement + LRU cache + tests
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-database.ts. openExtensionDb(dbPath): Database.Database opens better-sqlite3 with the packages/db house pragmas (journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, busy_timeout=5000, foreign_keys=ON — mirror packages/db/src/index.ts:10-47). Import better-sqlite3 directly (native addon already externalized; confine the import to apps/server service code, no client/FSD import — mirrors @dorkos/db). ensureMeta(db) creates `_dork_meta(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL)` if absent. getSchemaVersion(db)/setSchemaVersion(db, v) read/write the 'schema_version' row (absent → 0). measureBytes(db) = db.pragma('page_count', {simple:true}) * db.pragma('page_size', {simple:true}). Maintain an LRU cache keyed by absolute dbPath (bounded, e.g. 32) that closes evicted handles; closeAll() for shutdown. TSDoc on all exports. ACCEPTANCE: opening a fresh :memory: or tmp-file DB applies all four pragmas (assert via db.pragma reads); ensureMeta is idempotent; getSchemaVersion returns 0 on a fresh DB and round-trips after setSchemaVersion; measureBytes grows after an insert; the LRU closes an evicted handle (a closed handle throws on use). Add __tests__/extension-database.test.ts using a fresh tmpdir per case. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-database.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 1 (connection), §4c (quota measurement). Depends on 1.2 (resolveDbPath). Parallel with 1.4-adjacent P2 tasks 2.1/2.3.
tasks.activeForm: Building the per-extension SQLite connection and meta/quota helpers

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P1] Migrator: apply pending migrations in one SQLite transaction (transactional rollback, NO file backups), forward-only downgrade refusal + tests
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-migrator.ts. runMigrations(dbPath, migrations: StorageMigration[]): MigrationResult. Steps (spec §5): (1) open store.db via extension-database, ensureMeta, applied = getSchemaVersion; (2) if applied > max(migrations.version) → return { ok:false, code:'SCHEMA_DOWNGRADE' } WITHOUT mutating; (3) pending = migrations.filter(m=>m.version>applied) sorted ascending; if empty return { ok:true, appliedThrough: applied }; (4) db.transaction(() => { for each pending: db.exec(m.up); setSchemaVersion(m.version) })() — one SQLite transaction; (5) on throw: the transaction auto-rolls back (SQLite DDL is transactional — schema, data, and schema_version all revert), return { ok:false, code:'MIGRATION_FAILED', version, message }; on success return { ok:true, appliedThrough: maxVersion }. CRITICAL — DO NOT copy/restore the DB file: a file-level backup of store.db alone is WAL-UNSAFE (committed rows can live in store.db-wal, so the copy silently misses them; restoring a stale main file while -wal/-shm sidecars persist corrupts the DB via WAL replay against the wrong base). The transaction IS the rollback mechanism; the migrator creates no .bak files (spec §5 'Deliberately no file-level backup/restore'). Provide a dryRun(migrations) that applies to a fresh :memory: DB and returns ok/err WITHOUT touching disk (for install-time validation). TSDoc on all exports. ACCEPTANCE: fresh DB applies 1..N and stamps _dork_meta=N; a re-run with the same migrations is a no-op (appliedThrough unchanged); adding version N+1 applies only the delta; the WAL-correctness case — insert committed rows, then run a v-next migration whose `up` is invalid SQL: it returns MIGRATION_FAILED, no new tables exist, schema_version is unchanged, AND the previously committed rows are still readable afterwards; assert no store.db.bak* file is ever created in the DB dir; applied>max returns SCHEMA_DOWNGRADE and mutates nothing; dryRun catches a bad migration without creating any file. Add __tests__/extension-migrator.test.ts with tmpdir DBs. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-migrator.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 5 (migration execution & rollback — transactional, no file backup), ADR-0304 (the previous-state-intact guarantee, delivered here by SQLite atomicity). Depends on 1.1 (StorageMigration type), 1.3 (connection + meta).
tasks.activeForm: Building the transactional extension schema migrator

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P2] SQL guard: keyword pre-filter + Statement.readonly authority (defeats WITH…DELETE) + WITH RECURSIVE rejection + tests
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/extensions/sql-guard.ts implementing BOTH layers of spec §4b. LAYER 1 — pure keyword pre-filter, guardStatement(sql, surface: 'query' | 'run'): void (throws ExtensionDbError code 'STATEMENT_REJECTED' with a reason message that never echoes values): reject any input containing a statement separator outside string/comment (multi-statement); leading-keyword check — surface 'query' allows SELECT or WITH, surface 'run' allows INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/WITH (WITH-prefixed DML is legal SQLite); DENYLIST rejected on both surfaces as the leading keyword: ATTACH, DETACH, PRAGMA, VACUUM, ALTER, DROP, CREATE, REINDEX, ANALYZE, BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, SAVEPOINT, RELEASE; reject WITH RECURSIVE on BOTH surfaces (token scan outside strings/comments — the compute-DoS guard, spec §4c: a recursive CTE returns one row past every volume cap while freezing the synchronous event loop). Tokenize enough to ignore keywords inside quoted strings/comments. LAYER 2 — the AUTHORITY: export enforceSurface(stmt: { readonly: boolean }, surface): void, called by the executor AFTER db.prepare(sql): for surface 'query' reject unless stmt.readonly === true (better-sqlite3's binding of sqlite3_stmt_readonly — the engine's own verdict); for surface 'run' reject when stmt.readonly === true (a read posing as a write is a contract error). The keyword scan is a cheap pre-filter for fast errors and engine-inexpressible rules ONLY — it CANNOT enforce the read contract, because SQLite permits 'WITH cte AS (SELECT 1) DELETE FROM t', which passes any leading-token check yet compiles as a writer. TSDoc on exports. ACCEPTANCE (layer 1, pure): 'SELECT * FROM t' passes query, fails run; 'INSERT INTO t VALUES(?)' passes run, fails query; 'SELECT 1; DROP TABLE t' rejected on both (multi-statement); 'ATTACH DATABASE ...', 'PRAGMA user_version', 'DROP TABLE t', 'CREATE TABLE t(...)', 'BEGIN' all rejected on both; 'WITH RECURSIVE r(n) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT n+1 FROM r) SELECT count(*) FROM r' rejected on both; a SELECT whose string literal contains 'DROP TABLE' still passes (keyword-in-literal not misfired). ACCEPTANCE (layer 2, against a real :memory: better-sqlite3 handle): THE LOAD-BEARING CASE — prepare('WITH x AS (SELECT 1) DELETE FROM contacts') passes the layer-1 pre-filter for 'query' but enforceSurface rejects it because stmt.readonly === false; prepare('WITH x AS (SELECT 1) SELECT * FROM x') passes both layers on 'query'; a plain SELECT has stmt.readonly === true and passes. Each case a purpose comment, no always-pass. Add __tests__/sql-guard.test.ts. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/sql-guard.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server lint. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 4b (pre-filter + readonly authority), §4c (WITH RECURSIVE), §Security. No dependencies; parallel with all of P1.
tasks.activeForm: Building the SQL guard pre-filter and readonly authority

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P2] ExtensionDataAPI types (@dorkos/extension-api) + wire DTOs (@dorkos/shared) + ExtensionDbError
tasks.description: Add the author-facing contract and wire types (spec §4a, §7). In packages/extension-api/src/: export type SqlValue = string | number | bigint | boolean | null | Uint8Array; interface RunResult { changes: number; lastInsertRowid: number | bigint }; interface SqlStep { sql: string; params?: SqlValue[] }; interface ExtensionDataAPI { query<T>(sql, params?): Promise<T[]>; run(sql, params?): Promise<RunResult>; transaction(steps: SqlStep[]): Promise<RunResult[]> }. Add `readonly data: ExtensionDataAPI` to ExtensionAPI (extension-api.ts) and to DataProviderContext (server-extension-api.ts). Export a typed ExtensionDbError class with a discriminated `code` union: 'STATEMENT_REJECTED' | 'QUOTA_EXCEEDED' | 'ROWS_TRUNCATED' | 'RESULT_TOO_LARGE' | 'SCHEMA_DOWNGRADE' | 'MIGRATION_FAILED' | 'NOT_FOUND'. In packages/shared/src/ add extension-db-dto.ts: Zod schemas QueryRequestDto { sql, params? }, QueryResultDto { rows: unknown[], truncated: boolean }, RunRequestDto, RunResultDto { changes, lastInsertRowid }, TransactionRequestDto { steps }, TransactionResultDto { results } — and add the subpath to packages/shared/package.json exports (24→25). TSDoc on all exports. NOTE: bigint is not JSON-serializable — the DTO encodes lastInsertRowid as a number when safe and a string tagged form otherwise; document the encoding. ACCEPTANCE: types compile and are exported from the barrels; DTOs round-trip parse a representative payload incl. null/number params; the new @dorkos/shared subpath resolves. Add __tests__ for the DTO round-trip incl. the bigint encoding. VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/extension-api typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared build && pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/__tests__/extension-db-dto.test.ts. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 4a, §7. No dependencies; parallel with all of P1.
tasks.activeForm: Defining the ExtensionDataAPI types and wire DTOs

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P2] Executor: query/run/transaction with row cap, byte cap, quota-on-write, typed errors + tests
tasks.description: Create apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-data-executor.ts — the single implementation both the routes (3.1) and ctx.data (3.2) call. Given a resolved dbPath + effective limits, expose query(dbPath, sql, params, limits), run(...), transaction(dbPath, steps, limits). Each: guardStatement (2.1) pre-filter first; prepare with better-sqlite3 (single-statement structural guard); then enforceSurface(stmt, surface) (2.1) — the Statement.readonly AUTHORITY that actually enforces the read contract (spec §4b layer 2); bind params positionally. query: fetch rows with the row cap applied — request cap+1 rows, if the count exceeds the cap return the first `cap` rows and truncated:true (the route/client turns truncated into ROWS_TRUNCATED, spec §4c); enforce the result byte cap on the serialized payload → RESULT_TOO_LARGE. run/transaction: wrap in db.transaction(); BEFORE commit, measureBytes (1.3) and if post-write size > effective quota → throw ExtensionDbError QUOTA_EXCEEDED so the transaction rolls back (assert the row count is unchanged after a rejected write). transaction runs all steps in one db.transaction() — all-or-nothing. Effective limits are passed in as MODULE-CONSTANT DEFAULTS for now (DEFAULT_MAX_ROWS=1000, HARD_MAX_ROWS=10000, DEFAULT_MAX_RESULT_BYTES=8MB, DEFAULT_QUOTA_BYTES=25MB, clamp manifest.storage.quotaBytes to the host max); task 4.3 rewires these to config. TSDoc on exports; keep functions under the complexity limits (extract helpers). ACCEPTANCE (all against tmp/:memory: DBs with a migrated schema): a parameterized INSERT then SELECT round-trips bound values incl. null and Uint8Array; a param value of "'); DROP TABLE contacts;--" is stored verbatim as data and executes nothing (table still present); query('WITH x AS (SELECT 1) DELETE FROM contacts') throws STATEMENT_REJECTED via the readonly authority and deletes nothing (row count unchanged); query('WITH RECURSIVE ...') throws STATEMENT_REJECTED from the pre-filter; a SELECT over cap+ rows returns exactly `cap` rows + truncated:true; an oversized result throws RESULT_TOO_LARGE; a write crossing the quota throws QUOTA_EXCEEDED and leaves the row count unchanged; a transaction whose 2nd step fails reverts the 1st. Add __tests__/extension-data-executor.test.ts, each case a purpose comment. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-data-executor.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 4b/4c, §Security. Depends on 1.3 (connection/measureBytes), 2.1 (guard), 2.3 (types).
tasks.activeForm: Building the query/run/transaction executor with resource caps

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P3] Server routes POST /db/query|run|transaction (scoped, existence-checked) + integration tests
tasks.description: Add three routes to apps/server/src/routes/extensions.ts (mounted /api/extensions, index.ts:1003-1006): POST /:id/db/query, /:id/db/run, /:id/db/transaction. Each handler: validate SAFE_EXT_ID (400 if malformed); require extensionManager.get(id) (404 if absent) — same authorization model as the blob routes; parse the body with the QueryRequestDto/RunRequestDto/TransactionRequestDto (2.3) → 400 on invalid; resolve the DB via resolveDbPath (1.2); call the executor (2.2) with the effective limits (manifest.storage?.quotaBytes clamped to host default); map ExtensionDbError codes to HTTP (STATEMENT_REJECTED/ROWS_TRUNCATED/RESULT_TOO_LARGE/QUOTA_EXCEEDED → 4xx with the typed body, NOT_FOUND → 404) and unexpected errors to 500 (log, don't leak). Return QueryResultDto { rows, truncated } / RunResultDto / TransactionResultDto. Express 5 semantics: req.body is undefined on an empty POST — treat as 400. ACCEPTANCE (supertest against a real Express app + a real tmp SQLite seeded via the migrator, extension manager seeded with a discovered fixture extension): a query round-trips rows; a mutating statement sent to /db/query returns STATEMENT_REJECTED 4xx; an unknown :id → 404; a malformed :id → 400; a transaction commits atomically; a quota-crossing write → QUOTA_EXCEEDED 4xx and the row count is unchanged. Add __tests__/extension-db-routes.integration.test.ts. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/extension-db-routes.integration.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server lint. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 7 (bridge — client extensions). Depends on 2.2 (executor), 2.3 (DTOs), 1.4 (migrator, to seed the fixture schema).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the scoped /db/* server routes and integration tests

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P3] Wire DataProviderContext.data in-process for server extensions + tests
tasks.description: In apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-server-api-factory.ts, add `data` to the DataProviderContext built for server-side extensions, bound directly to the executor (2.2) in-process (no HTTP) — mirroring how the existing `storage`/`secrets`/`settings` bind there. Resolve the extension's dbPath via resolveDbPath (1.2) using the extension's id + scope; pass the effective limits from the extension's manifest.storage. The three methods (query/run/transaction) delegate to the executor and surface ExtensionDbError unchanged. TSDoc. ACCEPTANCE: a server extension's ctx.data.run + ctx.data.query round-trip against its own store.db; the DB path is the extension's own (isolation — a second fixture extension cannot see the first's rows); errors propagate as ExtensionDbError. Add __tests__/extension-server-api-factory.data.test.ts. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-server-api-factory.data.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 7 (server extensions — ctx.data in-process). Depends on 2.2 (executor), 2.3 (types), 1.2 (resolveDbPath). Parallel with 3.1.

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P3] Client api.data on ExtensionAPI (fetch to scoped routes) + client tests
tasks.description: Implement api.data in apps/client/src/layers/features/extensions/model/extension-api-factory.ts (createExtensionAPI, lines 38-216) following the existing loadData/saveData seam — raw fetch via extensionApiUrl(`/extensions/${extId}/db/<op>`) (extension-api-url.ts), POST JSON { sql, params } / { steps }. Parse responses with the @dorkos/shared DTOs (2.3): query throws ExtensionDbError 'ROWS_TRUNCATED' when body.truncated is true (silent truncation is impossible); a non-2xx maps to a typed ExtensionDbError built from the error body (helper toExtensionDbError(res)). Expose `data: { query, run, transaction }` on the returned api object; no new cleanup registration needed. TSDoc. NOTE (A-WEB-FIRST): this is HTTP-only, exactly like loadData/saveData — no DirectTransport path; the api.data signatures are stable so a later Transport promotion is a wire-path swap. ACCEPTANCE (RTL + jsdom, fetch mocked): query POSTs { sql, params } to /extensions/<id>/db/query and returns rows; run/transaction POST to their routes and return the parsed result; body.truncated:true throws ROWS_TRUNCATED; a 4xx maps to a typed ExtensionDbError with the server code. Add __tests__/extension-api-factory.data.test.ts. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/features/extensions/model/__tests__/extension-api-factory.data.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 7 (client bridge). Depends on 2.3 (types/DTOs), 3.1 (route contract to call).

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P4] Run the migrator on extension enable/load; disable + surface on failure + tests
tasks.description: Hook the migrator (1.4) into the extension enable/load path in apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-server-lifecycle.ts (the initialize path, ~lines 46-97) so that when an extension with a manifest.storage block is enabled, runMigrations(resolveDbPath(id), manifest.storage.migrations) runs BEFORE the server entry initializes. On MigrationResult.ok===false set the extension status to 'error' with the migration message (the same status a failed server-init sets today) and DO NOT initialize the server entry — the extension does not go active on a broken/downgraded schema. On ok, proceed. Ensure a migration failure for one extension does not affect others (separate files; catch per-extension). TSDoc. ACCEPTANCE: enabling a fixture extension with valid storage.migrations creates store.db at the resolved path with the tables + _dork_meta stamped; enabling one whose v2 migration has invalid SQL leaves status 'error', the message surfaced, store.db in its pre-migration state via the transactional rollback (v1 tables + rows intact, schema_version still 1, no .bak files), and the server entry NOT initialized; a second healthy extension enabled in the same pass is unaffected. Add __tests__/extension-server-lifecycle.migrations.test.ts. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-server-lifecycle.migrations.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 6 (lifecycle — install/first enable/update). Depends on 1.4 (migrator).
tasks.activeForm: Hooking the migrator into the extension enable lifecycle

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P4] Extend uninstall --purge to remove extension-data/:id (close the purge gap) + tests
tasks.description: In apps/server/src/services/marketplace/flows/uninstall.ts, when req.purge===true, additionally remove the per-extension data directories for every extension bundled in the package: for each bundled extension id, rm -rf resolveExtensionDataDir(id) at global scope (dorkHome/extension-data/:id) and, when req.projectPath is set, the local dir (<projectPath>/.dork/extension-data/:id). Enumerate bundled extension ids from the STAGED copy of the package — read .dork/extensions/*/extension.json (the discovery convention, extension-discovery.ts) under stagingPath after uninstall.ts moves the package dir aside, consistent with the existing uninstall flow (the staged copy is the authoritative snapshot of what was installed; do not pre-read the live dir before the move). Default (purge:false) behavior is UNCHANGED — extension-data survives (spec §6, existing behavior). Add the removed paths to UninstallResult (e.g. a purgedData: string[] field) so the CLI/UI can report them. Update contributing/marketplace-installs.md §4 to state --purge now also removes extension-data/:id. TSDoc. ACCEPTANCE: uninstall with purge:false leaves store.db in place; uninstall with purge:true removes extension-data/:id at global scope (and local when projectPath given); a package with two bundled extensions purges both; the removal is best-effort (a missing dir is not an error). Add cases to the existing uninstall test suite. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/marketplace/flows/__tests__/uninstall.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 6 (uninstall --purge), contributing/marketplace-installs.md §4. Depends on 1.2 (resolveExtensionDataDir). Parallel with 4.1, 4.3.

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P4] Global config fields (dataQuotaBytes / dataMaxRows / dataMaxResultBytes) + semver migration; wire executor to config
tasks.description: Add host-level limits to UserConfigSchema under an `extensions` block via the adding-config-fields skill: dataQuotaBytes (default 26214400 = 25MB), dataMaxRows (default 1000), dataMaxResultBytes (default 8388608 = 8MB). Follow contributing/configuration.md: extend the Zod schema (services/core/config-manager.ts), add defaults to USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS, add a semver-keyed CONFIG_MIGRATIONS entry (backfill*, idempotent, append-only at the next release version) that backfills the new block when absent, and document the fields in contributing/configuration.md. Then replace the module-constant defaults in the executor (2.2) and the routes (3.1)/ctx.data (3.2) effective-limit computation with configManager.get('extensions') values (manifest.storage.quotaBytes is clamped to config dataQuotaBytes). TSDoc. ACCEPTANCE: config schema accepts/omits the extensions block with correct defaults; the migration backfills an old config idempotently (running twice is a no-op); the executor uses the configured limits (a test lowering dataMaxRows to 5 truncates at 5). Add config-manager migration test + wire test. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/core/__tests__/config-manager.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 4c (resource limits), §Documentation; adding-config-fields skill. No hard dependency (executor 2.2 ships with constant defaults); lands the config wiring. Parallel with 4.1, 4.2.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the host data-limit config fields and wiring them

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P5] CRM-lite conformance test — contacts+pipeline+follow-up query, zero-escape-hatches proof
tasks.description: Add the acceptance-gate integration test apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/extension-data-crm-lite.conformance.test.ts driving the real /db/* routes (3.1) end-to-end. Use the exact manifest.storage from spec §8 (contacts + deals tables + indexes, migration v1) applied via the migrator; a fixture extension seeded in the extension manager. Steps: apply migration; INSERT several contacts and deals via parameterized run/transaction; run the spec §8 follow-up query (JOIN contacts+deals, WHERE next_follow_up <= ? AND stage = ?, GROUP BY, SUM(amount_cents), ORDER BY open_value DESC) with bound params; assert the ranked result (most valuable open negotiation first, only contacts due today-or-earlier). ZERO-ESCAPE-HATCHES ASSERTION: the test exercises ONLY api-equivalent /db/* calls — assert no data.json is written for the extension during the run (the blob path stays absent) and that every mutation/read went through the parameterized routes (no string-interpolated SQL in the test's own calls; params carry all values). Each assertion a purpose comment. VERIFY: pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/extension-data-crm-lite.conformance.test.ts && pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck. CITES: spec §Detailed Design 8 (CRM-lite validation) — this task IS the acceptance criterion. Depends on 3.1 (routes), 1.4 (migrator).
tasks.activeForm: Writing the CRM-lite conformance acceptance test

tasks.subject: [extension-data-layer] [P5] Developer guide + docs + example extension
tasks.description: Write contributing/extension-data-layer.md (writing-developer-guides skill): the per-extension storage model, the manifest `storage` block (with the CRM migration), the api.data / ctx.data surface, quotas + row/byte caps + typed errors, the migration lifecycle + rollback, uninstall/purge retention, and the HTTP-only (A-WEB-FIRST) boundary. Add it to contributing/INDEX.md. Add a user-facing docs page (docs/, Fumadocs, writing-for-humans) showing an extension storing + querying data with the CRM snippet. Ship a minimal example extension (or extend an existing fixture) that declares storage.migrations and calls api.data, referenced by the guide. Cross-link from contributing/marketplace-installs.md. TSDoc/prose only; no runtime code beyond the example. ACCEPTANCE: guide covers every section above and cites real paths; INDEX.md updated; docs page renders; the example extension's manifest validates against StorageDeclarationSchema (a test asserts this). VERIFY: pnpm --filter @dorkos/site build (docs render) && pnpm vitest run <example-manifest validation test>. CITES: spec §Documentation. Depends on 5.1 (docs describe the validated design). Parallel with 5.1 for drafting.
tasks.activeForm: Writing the developer guide, docs, and example extension
